original_image_tags: The reverse side of a document. The paper is off-white or cream-colored with visible aging, including a large, irregular brownish stain near the top. Two punched holes are visible on the right edge, indicating it was once in a binder. A small, rectangular piece of semi-transparent tape is affixed to the right side, partially covering a crease. In the upper left corner, there is a faded, partially legible stamp that reads "RECD - COPY & [REDACTED]". Below that is a clear blue ink stamp: "FBI DEC 23 11 17 AM '66". The main stamp, also in blue ink, is oriented vertically along the left side: "RECEIVED - DIRECTOR FBI DEC 22 5 26 PM '66". The rest of the page is blank.

elements.description: Two distinct ink stamps and one piece of translucent tape on a blank or aged paper background. Top stamp is partially legible, reading 'FBI' and 'DEC 23 11 17 AM '66'. Bottom stamp reads 'RECEIVED - DIRECTOR', 'F B I', and 'DEC 22 5 26 PM '66'. A rectangular piece of translucent tape is adhered to the right side of the page. The page shows yellowing and aging.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the same visual elements: two blue ink stamps with matching text (FBI DEC 23 11 17 AM '66 and RECEIVED - DIRECTOR FBI DEC 22 5 26 PM '66), one piece of translucent tape, and an aged paper background.
